hypothesis: If perturbed reference crystals and generative-model samples are evaluated with a DFT-anchored framework that combines relaxation-based RMSD and convex-hull SUN with property/latent metrics, then scores will decrease monotonically with increasing perturbation and will correctly rank models by the DFT stability (energy above hull) and relaxability of their outputs because first-principles simulations provide chemically faithful ground truth that heuristic metrics lack.
